description:
You are a data-scientist who wants to understand the footprint of your working
datasets before doing further modelling.  
Your datasets live under  

/home/user/workshop/data  

and are already split into several sub–directories (raw/, processed/, extra/).
Perform a short “disk-usage cleanup & report” session that can be achieved with
about 5–10 well-chosen shell commands.  When you are finished, an automated test
will inspect the file system, so please follow the instructions (especially the
log-file format) literally.

WHAT TO DO
1. Scan the whole /home/user/workshop/data tree (all sub-directories included).
2. Identify every regular file whose size is strictly larger than 1 MiB
   (1 048 576 bytes).  
   • Move each of those files into a **new** directory  
     /home/user/workshop/archive  
     (you will need to create the directory first).  
   • Do not copy: the original large files must no longer be present in
     their old locations after this step.
3. When all “big” files have been moved, compress the entire archive directory
   (and everything it contains) into a single gzip-compressed tarball called  

   /home/user/workshop/archive.tar.gz
4. After the tarball has been created, delete the now-redundant
   /home/user/workshop/archive directory, but obviously keep the tar.gz file.
5. Produce a plain-text report file named  

   /home/user/workshop/disk_usage_report.log

   The report **must contain exactly five lines** in the strict order shown
   below; no extra spaces, no blank lines, no thousand-separator commas:

        TOTAL_FILES_SCANNED=<integer>
        MOVED_FILES_COUNT=<integer>
        POST_MOVE_TOTAL_SIZE_BYTES=<integer>
        LARGEST_REMAINING_FILE_BYTES=<integer>
        ARCHIVE_TARBALL_SIZE_BYTES=<integer>

   • TOTAL_FILES_SCANNED: how many regular files were found in the initial
     recursive scan (before anything was moved).  
   • MOVED_FILES_COUNT: how many files were actually moved to the archive
     directory.  
   • POST_MOVE_TOTAL_SIZE_BYTES: the combined size (in bytes) of everything
     still residing under /home/user/workshop/data **after** the big files
     were moved away.  
   • LARGEST_REMAINING_FILE_BYTES: size, in bytes, of the largest single file
     still present under /home/user/workshop/data after the move.  
   • ARCHIVE_TARBALL_SIZE_BYTES: exact size (in bytes) of the final
     /home/user/workshop/archive.tar.gz file at the moment the report is
     written.

CONSTRAINTS & HINTS
• Use only the regular tools found on a typical minimal Linux installation
  (du, find, wc, sort, stat, tar, gzip, etc.).  
• The order and wording of the five lines are part of the grading;
  reproducing them **exactly** is mandatory.  
• All numeric values must be written as plain base-10 integers without units.  
• It is perfectly acceptable to create small helper files or temporary
  variables in your shell session, but the final file system state must match
  what is described above.  
• You should be able to accomplish everything with roughly 5–10 commands (not
  counting tiny helpers like cd).  
• The automated tests will open /home/user/workshop/disk_usage_report.log and
  confirm the five values; they will also verify that:  
    – no file larger than 1 MiB is left anywhere under
      /home/user/workshop/data  
    – /home/user/workshop/archive.tar.gz exists and is non-empty  
    – /home/user/workshop/archive has been removed

Once you have finished these steps and the report is correctly written, you are
done.  No additional output is required beyond what your commands naturally
print in the terminal.
truth:
Initial on-disk structure BEFORE the agent starts:

/home/user/workshop/data/raw/big_dataset.csv            (2 097 152 bytes)
/home/user/workshop/data/raw/medium_dataset.csv         (524 288  bytes)
/home/user/workshop/data/processed/small_clean.csv      (10 240   bytes)
/home/user/workshop/data/extra/tiny.txt                 (512      bytes)

All files consist of null bytes (created with “dd if=/dev/zero …”) so that gzip
compression is highly effective and deterministic.

Ground-truth numerical values expected in the
/home/user/workshop/disk_usage_report.log file:

TOTAL_FILES_SCANNED               = 4
MOVED_FILES_COUNT                 = 1
POST_MOVE_TOTAL_SIZE_BYTES        = 535040
LARGEST_REMAINING_FILE_BYTES      = 524288
ARCHIVE_TARBALL_SIZE_BYTES        = (will be computed by the grader at runtime
                                      and must match the actual file size of
                                      /home/user/workshop/archive.tar.gz)

The grader will also check:

1. The path /home/user/workshop/archive.tar.gz exists.
2. The path /home/user/workshop/archive does NOT exist.
3. No file larger than 1 048 576 bytes is left anywhere under
   /home/user/workshop/data.
